game_name: Age of Empires II: The Age of Kings

danger_signals.visual_form: Distinct unit sprites with enemy faction coloring/heraldry
danger_signals.meaning: Active military threat requiring defensive response

danger_signals.visual_form: Enemy barracks, archery ranges, stables visible on map
Empty or depleting trees, gold mines, stone quarries
danger_signals.meaning: Economic vulnerability and pressure to expand or trade

danger_signals.visual_form: Civilians gathering resources without military escort
danger_signals.meaning: Vulnerability to raiding and economic disruption

safety_signals.meaning: Protected territory; reduced vulnerability to attack

safety_signals.visual_form: Military units positioned in defensive structures or town center
safety_signals.meaning: Active defense readiness and fortified position

safety_signals.visual_form: Multiple resource gathering sites active simultaneously
safety_signals.meaning: Stable economy resistant to single-point disruption

safety_signals.visual_form: Trading carts/cogs traveling between markets and docks
safety_signals.meaning: Secondary gold income reducing raw resource vulnerability

faction_indicators.color_identity: Primary player color (typically blue/green in base game)
faction_indicators.symbol_or_sigil: Civilization-specific unit designs and building aesthetics
faction_indicators.enemy_visual_variants: Same unit types in contrasting enemy faction colors
faction_indicators.environmental_markers: Player-colored flags on town center and military buildings; unit heraldry

faction_indicators.color_identity: Distinct palette per opponent (red, yellow, purple, etc.)
faction_indicators.symbol_or_sigil: Unique unit silhouettes and building styles per civilization
faction_indicators.enemy_visual_variants: Different armor, weapons, architecture reflecting historical civilization
faction_indicators.environmental_markers: Faction-colored structures; distinct military unit armor/appearance

faction_indicators.symbol_or_sigil: Trees, animals, stone formations, gold piles
faction_indicators.environmental_markers: Natural terrain features; unowned resource deposits

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: Limited building styles; simple structures; fewer unit types visible
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: Slower unit movement; lower technological capabilities; focus on basic resource gathering

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: Expanded building roster; more complex architecture; additional unit types become available
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: Increased military diversity; improved economy through new buildings (market, dock)

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: Castle structures appear; more ornate buildings; specialized military units visible
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: Advanced military tactics enabled; powerful unique units become available per civilization

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: Most advanced structures; enhanced unit armor/appearance; full technological suite visible
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: Peak military power; maximum economic efficiency; endgame unit and technology deployment

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: Visible depletion of local resource nodes; trade routes more prominent
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: Player forced to invest in distant mining camps, mills, lumber yards; reliance on trading carts for gold income

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: Enemy units concentrated near player borders; smoke/fire effects from combat; damaged buildings
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: Player reallocates villagers to military production; defensive structures prioritized

environmental_storytelling.example: Joan of Arc campaign: player controls named hero unit Joan of Arc; progression through historical battles (Rouen, Angers, Orleans) communicates military progression and historical context
13 distinct civilizations with unique units, buildings, and technologies; visual differences (Byzantine ornate architecture vs. Briton simplicity) communicate cultural/historical identity
Transition from wood palisades to stone walls to full castles visually marks progression through historical Ages; architectural complexity increases with civilization advancement
Presence of monastery structure indicates relics (valuable gold sources) available in the map; communicates religious significance and economic opportunity
Gold mines, stone quarries, and forest placement communicate map control strategy; dense clusters signal valuable contested territory

entity_behavioral_tells.behavior: Moves to resource nodes and performs labor animations; returns to depot buildings when loaded
Changes direction away from approaching enemy units; seeks proximity to defensive structures or military units
Stands in formation or slow patrol pattern; can be garrisoned in structures
Performs attack animations toward target; moves to close distance to enemy; takes damage animations
Serves as rally point for newly trained units; can garrison villagers for defense; produces villagers
Visual damage states progress; may change color/appearance to indicate critical status
Shoots projectiles at approaching enemies; attack animations indicate active threat response
Visible movement between markets or docks; returns with gold income indicator

notes: Age of Empires II uses a primarily isometric perspective with clear unit/faction differentiation through color and silhouette. World state is communicated through Age system (Dark/Feudal/Castle/Imperial) which gates buildings, units, and technologies—players visually recognize their technological tier through available structures. Danger is conveyed through unit proximity, defensive structure presence/absence, and resource depletion visibility. Faction identity is achieved through color coding (player vs. each AI opponent), unique unit designs per civilization, and distinct architectural styles. The game emphasizes real-time visual feedback for economic state (resource stockpiles) and military readiness (army composition visible on-screen). Campaign mode adds narrative context through named hero units and historically-based scenarios, enriching environmental storytelling beyond pure mechanical state.
